name: Clover Club

spec.method_detail: Dry shake first. Add ice and shake again. Double strain into chilled coupe.

history: Named for the Clover Club, a Philadelphia gentlemen's literary club that met at the Bellevue-Stratford Hotel before Prohibition. The cocktail predates Prohibition and appears in pre-1920 bar manuals. It fell out of fashion for decades — egg drinks did in general — before Julie Reiner revived it with her famous NYC bar of the same name.
